message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): URL percent-encoding passes through verbatim (tick 400)

Added `distributed_http_client_percent_encoded_url_passes_through` —
pins that `DistributedHttpClient::get` transmits percent-encoded
characters in the URL request-target exactly as given. No
double-encoding, no decoding, no re-normalization.

Why matters: the caller owns URL encoding. Distributed RPC callsites
commonly percent-encode query-string values (e.g. for API keys with
special characters, UUIDs with hyphens, etc.). A client that
double-encoded `%20` to `%2520` or decoded `%20` back to a literal
space would silently break every such callsite.

Test URL:
  /api/v1/search?q=hello%20world&filter=a%2Bb
  - %20 is a space (valid percent-encoded form)
  - %2B is a plus sign (valid percent-encoded form)

Server asserts the received first line:
  1. Contains "%20" — pass-through preserved.
  2. Contains "%2B" — pass-through preserved.
  3. Does NOT contain "%2520" — no double-encoding regression.

Tick 400 milestone tick — celebrating 30 HTTP client contract tests
in this session. Each test pins a specific production-observable
contract so regressions fire with focused diagnostics.

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 20s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
